taskSummary: Prepare the m6 chain-001 handoff design so the full orchestrator -> architect -> claude-code path can be audited without missing fields, unclear ownership, or undocumented assumptions.
acceptanceCriteria: Both hops use the same requestId and the same minimum field set.
routeReason explains why the orchestrator stops, why architect is next, and what risk is reduced.
The resulting chain is specific enough for s4 verification without re-deriving hidden context.
constraints: Do not expand the sample beyond the minimum executable handoff shape.
Preserve the chain-001 scope and keep the handoff traceable.
Do not modify files outside the approved dry-run artifact paths.
artifactsIn: user request: generate a complete chain-001 sample and execution proof
artifactsOut: implementation sequencing notes for the next hop
risk and rollback notes for the handoff boundary
routeReason: The orchestrator should stop here because the chain sample still needs its contract shape normalized before implementation-style execution begins. The architect is the right next owner because this step needs a stable field set, clear artifact boundaries, and a reusable chain structure. Escalating now reduces the risk of field drift, missing audit data, and a proof that cannot be replayed by s4.
riskNotes: A mismatched field set would make the chain hard to validate.
A vague routeReason would weaken the audit trail.
If the handoff boundary is unclear, s4 could not resume the chain safely.
